output.summary: Restructure the desktop header layout so the logo spans both rows (utility row + nav row). The current code places the nav items <nav> OUTSIDE the flex row that contains the logo, so the logo can only stretch to match the utility row height. In Figma, the logo is a direct flex sibling of a right column that contains BOTH the utility row and the nav items row in a flex-col gap-24. The fix moves the desktop nav items into the right column on desktop, while keeping the mobile collapsible nav behavior unchanged. Also reverts self-center back to self-stretch and preserves the text-white fix.

figmaStructure.description: Figma node 441:28858 (Navigation) uses 'flex items-center justify-between px-32 py-16 rounded-[25px]'. It has two direct children: (1) Logo container at 57.69px tall, and (2) Right column at w-833px as 'flex flex-col gap-24 items-end'. The right column contains the utility row (search + separator + SEP link) and the nav items row. The logo vertically centers against the combined height of both rows via items-center on the parent. The overall nav bar is 98.2px tall.

currentCodeStructure.description: The current code has: (A) a 'nav bar inner' div with 'flex items-center justify-between' containing the logo, the right column (utility row only), and mobile toggle. (B) A separate <nav> element BELOW this div containing the nav items with 'md:mt-6 md:items-end'. Because the <nav> is outside the flex row, the logo cannot stretch to match the combined height of utility + nav rows.
currentCodeStructure.problem: The logo's flex parent only contains the utility row on its right side. The nav items are a separate sibling below. So self-stretch on the logo only stretches it to match the utility row height, not the full two-row layout shown in Figma.

fileChanges.description: Restructure desktop layout so the logo spans both rows by moving the desktop nav items into the right column
fileChanges.details:
1. REVERT logo class from 'self-center' back to 'self-stretch' (line 70). Keep 'text-white'. The full logo class should be: 'block self-stretch text-white w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full **:object-contain shrink-0 max-md:**:max-h-[26px] md:**:max-h-[58px] focus-visible:outline-dotted focus-visible:outline-2 focus-visible:outline-white focus-visible:outline-offset-2'. 2. RESTRUCTURE the desktop right column: Currently the right column (line 77, 'hidden md:flex flex-col gap-6 items-end shrink') only contains the utility row. It needs to ALSO contain the desktop nav items row. Change its gap from gap-6 to gap-6 (24px = gap-6, matching Figma's gap-24px variable which is spacing-scale/6). 3. MOVE the desktop nav items: The
element (line 140-195) currently renders the nav
    for both desktop and mobile. For desktop, the nav items row needs to appear INSIDE the right column. The cleanest approach: duplicate the desktop nav items
      inside the right column (hidden on mobile via md:flex, the existing
        in
        already has the right classes), and hide the
        's
          on desktop. HOWEVER, since the $children variable can only be output once, a better approach is: - Keep the
          as-is for mobile (it already has max-md: collapsible behavior) - On desktop, restructure so the
          is INSIDE the flex row alongside the logo. This means the 'nav bar inner' div needs to wrap both the logo AND a right-side div that contains (a) utility row and (b) the
          with nav items. - The
          already has 'md:mt-6 md:items-end' — remove 'md:mt-6' since the gap will be handled by the parent flex-col gap-6. CONCRETE STRUCTURAL CHANGE in PHP: - The current structure is:
          logo
          utility row
          ... nav items ...
          - The NEW structure should be:
          logo
          utility row nav items row (desktop only, extracted from
          )
          ... mobile nav (collapsible, hidden on desktop) ...
          For the nav items row inside the right column, render the same $children
            but ONLY on desktop. Since $children can only be echoed once, use this approach: - Move the ENTIRE
            element inside the right column div, AFTER the utility row div. - The
            already handles both desktop and mobile via responsive classes. - The right column div changes from 'hidden md:flex' to 'md:flex flex-col gap-6 items-end shrink' (remove 'hidden' since
            handles its own mobile visibility). - BUT the mobile toggle button needs to remain OUTSIDE this right column (it's a sibling at the same level as the logo). ACTUAL CLEANEST APPROACH: - Wrap the right column AND the
            together in a single flex-col container that is the logo's sibling. - On desktop this container shows as flex-col with gap-6, containing utility row + nav. - On mobile, utility row is hidden, nav is collapsible. FINAL STRUCTURE:
            logo (self-stretch text-white)
            utility row (desktop only, unchanged)
            ... (remove md:mt-6, it's handled by parent gap-6) ...
            NOTE: Change the parent from 'items-center' to 'items-stretch' so the logo naturally stretches to the full height of the right column. The logo's self-stretch then works correctly. The
            element needs class adjustment: remove 'md:mt-6' (replaced by parent's md:gap-6). Keep all other classes.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the same structural changes in the editor TSX component
fileChanges.details:
Apply the same restructuring as PHP:
1. Revert logo classes from 'self-center' to 'self-stretch' in both ImageDropUploader (line 500) and logo <a> (line 511). Keep 'text-white'.
2. Change 'nav bar inner' div (line 490) from 'flex items-center justify-between' to 'flex items-stretch justify-between'.
3. Move the <nav> element (lines 602-685) to be INSIDE the right column div, after the utility row div.
4. Change the right column div (line 518) from 'hidden md:flex flex-col gap-6 items-end shrink' to 'flex flex-col md:gap-6 items-end shrink min-w-0' (remove 'hidden', add 'min-w-0' to prevent overflow).
5. Remove 'md:mt-6' from the <nav> className (line 605).
6. Keep the mobile toggle button as a sibling of the logo and right column (it stays at the same level).

designDecisions.question: How should the logo span both rows (utility + nav)?
designDecisions.recommendation: Move the <nav> element inside the right column div so it becomes a flex-col sibling of the utility row. Change the parent from items-center to items-stretch. The logo with self-stretch will then naturally fill the combined height of utility row + gap + nav row, matching the Figma layout where the logo is a direct sibling of the two-row right column.
designDecisions.evidence: Figma node 441:28858 shows the Navigation container as 'flex items-center justify-between' with two children: (1) logo at 57.69px tall, (2) right column as 'flex flex-col gap-24' containing utility row and nav row. The logo vertically centers against the combined ~66px content height (utility row + 24px gap + nav row). Total nav bar: 98.2px with 16px padding top/bottom.

designDecisions.question: Should we use items-center or items-stretch on the nav bar inner flex container?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use items-stretch. While Figma shows items-center, the practical effect is the same because: (a) the logo with self-stretch and max-h constraints will size correctly either way, and (b) items-stretch ensures the logo container fills the full height of the right column. The visual difference is negligible since the logo image is constrained by max-h-[58px] and object-contain. Using items-stretch is the simplest way to achieve the 'logo spans both rows' requirement without needing explicit height calculations.
designDecisions.evidence: The Figma uses items-center on node 441:28858, but in the implementation items-stretch is needed because the logo's actual visible size is controlled by the child constraints (**:max-h-[58px], **:object-contain). The <a> tag stretching to full height just means the clickable area spans both rows, which is good UX for a logo link.

designDecisions.question: Logo text-white for inline SVG currentColor visibility
designDecisions.recommendation: Preserve the text-white class added in revision 1. This is confirmed correct — inline SVGs use currentColor and need text-white on their container to be visible on the dark background.
designDecisions.evidence: Figma shows white logo on charcoal background. Previous revision correctly identified and fixed this.

designDecisions.question: Should the right column gap be gap-6 (24px) matching Figma?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use md:gap-6 on the right column. The current code already uses gap-6 on this div. Figma uses gap-24px (spacing-scale/6 = 24px) which maps to Tailwind gap-6. Apply this only on md: breakpoint since on mobile the utility row is hidden and the nav is collapsible.

mobileConsiderations.description: On mobile, the layout is fundamentally different: the utility row is hidden (md:hidden), the nav is a collapsible panel, and the logo is small (26px max-h). The restructuring must not break mobile behavior. The <nav> element's max-md: collapsible classes (max-h-0, opacity-0, etc.) must continue to work. The mobile toggle button must remain visible and functional.
risks: Moving <nav> inside the right column could break mobile toggle visibility if the right column has 'hidden' on mobile. Solution: remove 'hidden' from the right column, let child elements handle their own mobile visibility.
The mobile toggle button must remain a direct sibling at the same flex level as the logo, not nested inside the right column. Keep it as a third child of the nav bar inner div.
